summary.intent: Implement a series of security hardening measures for the Moltis web application, starting from GitHub issue #681 (disable terminal option) and expanding to comprehensive remote access security improvements including brute-force protection, account lockout, API key scope enforcement, and audit logging.
summary.outcome: All three security work batches were completed and pushed to PR #682: terminal disable feature with env var override, LoginGuard with IP banning and account lockout, and a 9-issue hardening pass (setup code expiry/brute-force guard, API key scope enforcement, HSTS/CSP headers, session limits, HMAC-SHA256 API key hashing, 12-char password minimum, and auth audit log). A second-pass review then fixed WebSocket bearer token scope propagation, password change brute-force guard, timing side channel in verify_password, API key audit logging, higher-entropy setup codes, and TLS startup warning.

repo: Config is structured as MoltisConfig → ServerConfig (top-level [server]) and tools.web / tools.X for feature-specific config; terminal_enabled belongs under [server], not [web]
Frontend receives server state via a 'gon' pattern (JSON injected into HTML), similar to how voice_enabled and graphql_enabled are gated
The codebase uses a beads issue tracker (not GitHub Issues directly) for task management with claim/close workflow before committing
API key scopes are stored in the DB but were not enforced anywhere — the scope fields existed but check_auth discarded them
WebSocket RPC already had per-method scope enforcement via authorize_method/MethodContext::client_scopes, but bearer token auth via HTTP headers bypassed scope extraction

Two separate copies of websocket_header_authenticated exist: one in httpd/src/server.rs (for RPC WS) and one in web/src/terminal.rs (for terminal WS) — terminal version is all-or-nothing so bool is sufficient
unsafe code (set_var/remove_var) is denied by a project-wide lint, so env var testing requires integration tests or workarounds
hmac is already a workspace dependency but was not in the auth crate's Cargo.toml
Migration files live in crates/gateway/migrations/ with timestamp-prefixed filenames
The startup banner/TLS warning is in crates/httpd/src/server.rs, not in the gateway crate where TLS config lives

code.finding: WS handler tracks api_key_scopes from the JSON handshake params but was not propagating scopes from HTTP header bearer token auth — header_identity needed to be returned from websocket_header_authenticated instead of a bool
AuthIdentity was constructed without a scopes field in multiple places; all constructors needed updating when the field was added
verify_password returned early without doing Argon2 work when no password was set, creating a ~0ms vs ~100ms timing side channel; fixed with a dummy hash constant
require_config_access only took &AppState; to enforce API key scopes on config_save/restart, needed to add identity parameter extracted via axum::Extension<AuthIdentity>
GatewayInner needed a setup_code_created_at: Instant field to track setup code age for 30-minute expiry
IpRecord needed a last_failure_at: Instant field to allow cleanup of stale failure counters that had no ban — without it, single-failure IPs accumulated forever (memory leak)

workflow: Run clippy after every compilation — collapsible-if warnings were consistently caught and required fixing before commit
Background Bash tasks (run_in_background) are useful for long compilation/test runs; the notification system allows continuing other work
When axum extractors don't compose well across different State types (AuthState vs AppState), use axum::Extension<T> to read middleware-inserted request extensions from handlers
Test passwords need updating when changing validation minimums — integration tests use hardcoded passwords that may fall below new thresholds
The unsafe set_var/remove_var approach for env var tests is blocked by project lints; env var-dependent logic should be tested via integration tests instead

friction: axum extractor incompatibility between AuthState and AppState required multiple approach pivots to implement scope enforcement on config routes — Extension<AuthIdentity> was the eventual solution after RequireAdmin extractor failed due to FromRef bounds
Two separate websocket_header_authenticated copies caused confusion about which needed updating for scope propagation
cleanup() test in LoginGuard failed because IpRecord with consecutive_failures > 0 but no ban was retained (correct behavior) — required adding last_failure_at field and stale-entry expiry logic
CUDA crates cause clippy/test failures on macOS; all test runs required --exclude flags for CUDA-dependent crates
unsafe set_var/remove_var denied by project lint, preventing direct env var unit tests for MOLTIS_TERMINAL_DISABLED
open_items: Setup code entropy improvement (6 digits → 8 alphanumeric) increases search space but with 30 attempts per lockout window still only ~1:30,000 odds — consider further hardening for truly public deployments
Legacy API keys use plain SHA-256 (no salt) and continue to work via fallback in verify_api_key — migration path to force re-generation of old keys not implemented
Read-only config endpoints (config_get, config_validate, config_template) do not enforce operator.admin scope — only write endpoints are scope-gated
No audit log entries for passkey registration/removal events, only login success/failure
No per-user failed attempt counter for distributed brute-force (only IP-based) — account lockout only triggers from 3 distinct IPs, not from total attempt volume
